issue.title: workspace-search buttons need type=button
issue.body: Component: workspace-search's `createBtn` private helper method. We are using Blockly inside a `<form>` element, and `createBtn` does not set the `<button>` to `type='button'`, so clicking a workspace-search button (next, previous, exit) erroneously causes a form submit. The reporter suggests setting `btn.type = 'button'` in the `createBtn` helper. Reproduction: nest a Blockly instance in a `<form>`, install workspace-search, ctrl-f, click any of the three buttons, and observe the form is submitted.

comments.body: Hi @admalledd thanks for filing! We'd accept a PR for this if you'd like to submit your fix. Thanks!

@maribethb due to the language of my current work contract, that is difficult, hence provided the sample diff/line change in the issue above. I can open issues, discuss fixes, etc, but am not allowed to submit code under my own authorship if it relates to my work.
@BenHenning, apologies for the delay. Also, thanks for the support and patience.
i want to work on this because it's a small but important fix that helps prevent bugs when using forms. Setting type="button" avoids accidental form submissions...

note: Live recheck at fetch time: OPEN, unassigned, labels help-wanted + good-first-issue intact; NO open/active cross-referenced PR. Five prior PRs (#2546/2547/2551/2563/2567, all by @viky-01) are all CLOSED unmerged — closed for contributor churn (opening new PRs instead of updating), an unsigned Google CLA, and a failing prettier format check, then inactivity; NOT a maintainer rejection of the fix (maintainers @maribethb/@BenHenning actively coached each attempt and confirmed the fix is correct). @uniiquecornnx expressed intent 2025-07-08 but never opened a PR and was never assigned (stale). Maintainer @maribethb curated the issue 2026-07-08 (removed type:bug), leaving it open for contribution.
